preamble: The velar fricative [ɣ] shifts to the voiceless velar fricative [x] through devoicing. This process eliminates the voicing feature while preserving the place and manner of articulation, and occurs in various phonological contexts including word-final position and before voiceless consonants.

examples.note: Devoicing of post-alveolar velar fricatives in the development from Vulgar Latin through Old Spanish to Modern Spanish /x/.
Devoicing of intervocalic velar fricatives in word-final and pre-consonantal positions in various German dialects.
Devoicing in word-final and post-consonantal environments, contributing to regional phonological variation.
